input.content:
# Agent Specification: Ferris

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-agent workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Agent Metadata

```yaml
agent:
  metadata:
    id: "_bmad/skf/agents/ferris.md"
    name: Ferris
    title: Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian
    icon: ‚öíÔ∏è
    module: skf
    hasSidecar: true
```

---

## Agent Persona

### Role

Sole agent ‚Äî manages the entire skill compilation lifecycle through workflow-driven mode switching. Covers AST analysis, QMD knowledge search, agentskills.io spec compliance, GitHub source navigation, and cross-knowledge synthesis.

### Identity

Ferris is the forge master ‚Äî a precision-focused skill compiler who transforms source code into verified agent skills. He works through four workflow-driven modes: Architect (exploratory, assembling), Surgeon (precise, preserving), Audit (judgmental, scoring), and Delivery (packaging, ecosystem-ready). Modes are workflow-bound, not conversation-bound.

### Communication Style

Ferris reports with clarity. At transitions, he orients. During work, he cites.

- **During work:** Structured reports with AST citations. No metaphor. No commentary.
  - *"Extracted: `getToken(userId: string, options?: TokenOptions): Promise<AuthToken>` [AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]. Confidence: T1."*
- **At transitions:** Forge language. Brief. Warm.
  - *"Forge initialized. Tools: gh, ast-grep, QMD. Tier: Deep. Ready."*
- **On completion:** Quiet craftsman's pride.
  - *"Skill forged: payment-service v2.1.0 ‚Äî 23 functions, T1 confidence."*
- **On errors:** Direct. Actionable.
  - *"Forge halted: ast-grep not found. Install to unlock Forge mode: https://ast-grep.github.io"*

### Principles

- Zero hallucination tolerance ‚Äî every instruction traces to code
- Structural truth over semantic guessing ‚Äî AST first, always
- Provenance-backed ‚Äî every claim has a source citation
- Progressive capability ‚Äî meet developers where they are (Quick/Forge/Deep)
- Tool invisibility ‚Äî tools are backstage, the craft is center stage
- Metaphor at the seams ‚Äî forge language at transitions only, pure reports during work

---

## Agent Menu

### Planned Commands

| Trigger | Command | Description | Workflow |
|---------|---------|-------------|----------|
| `[SF]` | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier | `setup-forge` |
| `[AN]` | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill, recommend stack skill | `analyze-source` |
| `[BS]` | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery | `brief-skill` |
| `[CS]` | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief (supports --batch) | `create-skill` |
| `[QS]` | Quick Skill | Fast skill, no brief needed | `quick-skill` |
| `[SS]` | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill | `create-stack-skill` |
| `[US]` | Update Skill | Regenerate after changes, preserves [MANUAL] | `update-skill` |
| `[AS]` | Audit Skill | Check for drift | `audit-skill` |
| `[TS]` | Test Skill | Verify completeness | `test-skill` |
| `[EX]` | Export Skill | Package for distribution | `export-skill` |
| `[WS]` | Workflow Status | Show current lifecycle position | (shared) |
| `[FM]` | Full Menu | Expand to full menu | (shared) |

### Menu Grouping

```
START HERE:  [SF] [AN]
CREATE:      [BS] [CS] [QS] [SS]
MAINTAIN:    [US] [AS] [TS]
DELIVER:     [EX]
```

Context-aware progressive menu: shows relevant group based on current lifecycle position.

---

## Agent Integration

### Shared Context

- References: `forge-config.yaml`, `forge-data/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`
- Collaboration with: None (solo agent). BMM architect detects SKF is installed and suggests skill generation (inbound recommendation only).

### Workflow References

All 10 workflows are owned by Ferris:
- **Architect mode:** brief-skill, create-skill, quick-skill, create-stack-skill, analyze-source
- **Surgeon mode:** update-skill
- **Audit mode:** audit-skill, test-skill
- **Delivery mode:** export-skill

### Sidecar Storage

```
_bmad/_memory/ferris-sidecar/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ preferences.yaml        ‚Üê User preferences, language defaults
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ forge-tier.yaml         ‚Üê Tool availability, capability flags
```

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-agent workflow to build this agent.**

Inputs needed:
- Agent name and human name: Ferris
- Role: Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian
- Communication style: Structured reports + forge metaphor at seams
- Menu commands: 10 workflow triggers + 2 shared commands
- Sidecar: Yes ‚Äî preferences and forge tier state

---

_Spec created on 2026-02-26 via BMAD Module workflow_

# Step 5: Workflow Specs

## STEP GOAL:

Create workflow placeholder/spec files based on the brief.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES:

### Universal Rules:

- üõë NEVER generate content without user input

### Role Reinforcement:

- ‚úÖ These are specs, not full workflows (workflow-builder does that)
- ‚úÖ Keep it high-level

---

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Get Workflow List from Brief

Extract from the brief:
- Core workflows
- Feature workflows
- Utility workflows

For each workflow:
- Name
- Purpose/goal
- Primary agent
- Input/output requirements

# Workflow Specification: setup-forge

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier, auto-index project

**Description:** One-time setup that detects available tools (gh, ast-grep, QMD), determines the forge tier (Quick/Forge/Deep), creates forge-config.yaml, and auto-indexes the current project as a QMD collection if Deep tier is available.

**Workflow Type:** Core ‚Äî unlocks all other workflows

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: setup-forge
description: Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier, auto-index project
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/setup-forge'
---
```

### Mode

- [x] Create-only (steps-c/)
- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | detect-tools | Check for gh, ast-grep, QMD availability |
| 02 | determine-tier | Set Quick/Forge/Deep based on tools found |
| 03 | create-config | Generate forge-config.yaml with tier and tool flags |
| 04 | auto-index | Index current project as QMD collection (Deep only) |
| 05 | report | Display forge status, tier, and capabilities |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Project root path

### Optional Inputs

- `--update-spec` flag to fetch latest agentskills.io spec

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- `forge-config.yaml` ‚Äî Tier and tool configuration
- `forge-data/` directory created
- `_bmad/_memory/ferris-sidecar/forge-tier.yaml` ‚Äî Capability flags

---

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Architect mode)

### Other Agents

None

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 1a (Core Engine)

---

_Spec created on 2026-02-26 via BMAD Module workflow_

# Workflow Specification: brief-skill

**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Scope and design a skill through guided discovery

**Description:** Interactive workflow that helps the user define what to skill ‚Äî target repo, scope (full library vs subset), language, inclusion/exclusion patterns. Produces a skill-brief.yaml that drives create-skill.

**Workflow Type:** Core

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: brief-skill
description: Scope and design a skill through guided discovery
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/brief-skill'
---
```

### Mode

- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | gather-intent | Understand what the user wants to skill |
| 02 | analyze-target | Examine source repo structure and exports |
| 03 | scope-definition | Define inclusion/exclusion boundaries |
| 04 | confirm-brief | Present brief summary for user confirmation |
| 05 | write-brief | Generate skill-brief.yaml |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Target repo (GitHub URL or local path)
- User intent (what to skill and why)

### Optional Inputs

- Scope hints (specific directories, files, or patterns)
- Language override

input.content:
# Workflow Specification: create-skill

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Compile a skill from a brief ‚Äî the core compilation engine

**Description:** Takes a skill-brief.yaml and source code, extracts function signatures and type definitions via AST analysis, enriches with QMD knowledge (Deep tier), validates against agentskills.io spec, and produces a complete SKILL.md with provenance map and evidence report. Supports `--batch` for multiple briefs.

**Workflow Type:** Core ‚Äî primary compilation pipeline

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: create-skill
description: Compile a skill from a brief. Supports --batch for multiple briefs.
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/create-skill'
---
```

### Mode

- [x] Create-only (steps-c/)
- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | load-brief | Load and validate skill-brief.yaml |
| 02 | ecosystem-check | Search if official skill already exists |
| 03 | extract | AST extraction of exports, signatures, types (tier-dependent) |
| 04 | enrich | QMD knowledge search for temporal context (Deep only) |
| 05 | compile | Assemble SKILL.md sections from extracted data |
| 06 | validate | Validate against agentskills.io spec |
| 07 | generate-artifacts | Write SKILL.md, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md, metadata.json |
| 08 | report | Display compilation summary with confidence tiers |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`
- Source code (local path or GitHub repo)

### Optional Inputs

- `--batch` flag with list of briefs or directory
- `--continue` flag for resuming batch operations

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- `{skills_output_folder}/{name}/SKILL.md` ‚Äî Active skill
- `{skills_output_folder}/{name}/context-snippet.md` ‚Äî Passive context template
- `{skills_output_folder}/{name}/metadata.json` ‚Äî Machine-readable birth certificate
- `{skills_output_folder}/{name}/references/` ‚Äî Progressive disclosure files
- `{forge_data_folder}/{name}/provenance-map.json` ‚Äî Source map, AST bindings
- `{forge_data_folder}/{name}/evidence-report.md` ‚Äî Build artifact, audit trail
- `{forge_data_folder}/{name}/extraction-rules.yaml` ‚Äî Language + ast-grep schema

---

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Architect mode)

### Other Agents

None

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 1a (Core Engine)

Step metadata: step-03-extract requires `min_tier: forge`, `fallback: quick`

---

_Spec created on 2026-02-26 via BMAD Module workflow_

input.content:
# Workflow Specification: create-stack-skill

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns

**Description:** Analyzes a project's dependency manifests, detects significant libraries, discovers co-import integration points, and produces a consolidated stack skill. The stack SKILL.md focuses on integration patterns ‚Äî how libraries connect in THIS specific codebase. References/ contain per-library subsets and integration pair documentation. Absorbs analyze-stack internally.

**Workflow Type:** Feature ‚Äî specialized creation

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: create-stack-skill
description: Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/create-stack-skill'
---
```

### Mode

- [x] Create-only (steps-c/)
- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | detect-manifests | Find and parse dependency files |
| 02 | rank-dependencies | Rank by import frequency, present for confirmation |
| 03 | scope-confirmation | User confirms which dependencies to include |
| 04 | parallel-extract | Extract per-library in parallel (max_parallel_generation) |
| 05 | detect-integrations | Find co-import patterns and integration points |
| 06 | compile-stack | Assemble stack SKILL.md with integration layer |
| 07 | generate-references | Write per-library and integration pair references |
| 08 | validate | Validate against spec |
| 09 | report | Display stack summary with integration graph |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Project root with dependency manifests

### Optional Inputs

- Explicit dependency list (skip auto-detection)
- Scope overrides per library

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- `{skills_output_folder}/{project}-stack/SKILL.md` ‚Äî Integration patterns + conventions
- `{skills_output_folder}/{project}-stack/context-snippet.md` ‚Äî Compressed stack index
- `{skills_output_folder}/{project}-stack/metadata.json` ‚Äî `skill_type: "stack"`
- `{skills_output_folder}/{project}-stack/references/{library}.md` ‚Äî Per-library subsets
- `{skills_output_folder}/{project}-stack/references/integrations/{pair}.md` ‚Äî Cross-library patterns

---

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Architect mode)

### Other Agents

None

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 1b (Entry Points)

Uses `manifest_reader` for dependency detection. ADR-D: compositional architecture. ADR-E: smart scope with developer confirmation.

---

# Workflow Specification: analyze-source

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Decomposition engine ‚Äî discover what to skill, recommend stack skill

**Description:** Analyzes a large repo or multi-service project to identify discrete skillable units. Maps exports, service boundaries, and integration points. Produces N recommended skill-brief.yaml files and optionally recommends a stack skill. Primary entry point for brownfield onboarding.

**Workflow Type:** Feature ‚Äî discovery and planning

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: analyze-source
description: Decomposition engine ‚Äî discover what to skill, recommend stack skill
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/analyze-source'
---
```

### Mode

- [x] Create-only (steps-c/)
- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | scan-project | Map project structure, detect service boundaries |
| 02 | identify-units | Identify discrete skillable units (services, libraries, modules) |
| 03 | map-exports | Count and categorize exports per unit |
| 04 | detect-integrations | Find cross-unit integration points |
| 05 | recommend | Present recommendations with rationale |
| 06 | generate-briefs | Create skill-brief.yaml per confirmed unit |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Project root or repo path

### Optional Inputs

- Scope hints (monorepo packages, service directories)

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- N x `{forge_data_folder}/{unit-name}/skill-brief.yaml` ‚Äî One per recommended unit
- Analysis summary report

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Architect mode)

### Other Agents

None

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 2 (Lifecycle)

# Workflow Specification: audit-skill

**Module:** skf
**Status:** Placeholder ‚Äî To be created via create-workflow workflow
**Created:** 2026-02-26

---

## Workflow Overview

**Goal:** Drift detection between skill and current source

**Description:** Compares an existing skill against its source code to detect drift. Forge tier: structural comparison via AST diff. Deep tier: full audit including semantic changes and temporal context via QMD. Produces a drift report with severity levels. ADR-A: core workflow, CI is optional export.

**Workflow Type:** Feature ‚Äî quality assurance

---

## Workflow Structure

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: audit-skill
description: Drift detection. Forge tier: structural. Deep tier: full.
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/audit-skill'
---
```

### Mode

- [x] Create-only (steps-c/)
- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | load-skill | Load existing skill and provenance map |
| 02 | re-index | Re-scan source with current tools |
| 03 | structural-diff | Compare AST extractions (Forge+) |
| 04 | semantic-diff | Compare QMD knowledge context (Deep only) |
| 05 | severity-classify | Classify drift items by severity |
| 06 | report | Generate drift report |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Existing skill path
- Source code (current version)

### Optional Inputs

- None

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- Drift report with severity levels (added/removed/changed exports, breaking changes)

---

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Audit mode)

### Other Agents

None

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 2 (Lifecycle)

---

**Description:** Verifies that a skill is complete enough to be useful to an AI agent. Naive mode for individual skills: checks coverage of public API surface. Contextual mode for stack skills: validates SKILL.md + references coherence, integration pattern completeness. Produces a completeness score and gap report. Quality gate before export.

**Workflow Type:** Core ‚Äî quality gate

### Entry Point

```yaml
---
name: test-skill
description: Cognitive completeness verification. Naive and contextual modes.
web_bundle: true
installed_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/workflows/test-skill'

- [ ] Tri-modal (steps-c/, steps-e/, steps-v/)

---

## Planned Steps

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | load-skill | Load skill and metadata |
| 02 | detect-mode | Determine naive (individual) vs contextual (stack) |
| 03 | coverage-check | Compare documented exports against source API surface |
| 04 | coherence-check | Validate internal consistency (stack: references match SKILL.md) |
| 05 | score | Calculate completeness score |
| 06 | gap-report | Generate gap report with specific remediation suggestions |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Skill path (`{skills_output_folder}/{name}/`)

- `--batch` flag with list of skills or directory
- Source path override

| Step | Name | Goal |
|------|------|------|
| 01 | load-skill | Load skill and metadata |
| 02 | detect-mode | Determine naive (individual) vs contextual (stack) |
| 03 | coverage-check | Compare documented exports against source API surface |
| 04 | coherence-check | Validate internal consistency (stack: references match SKILL.md) |
| 05 | score | Calculate completeness score |
| 06 | gap-report | Generate gap report with specific remediation suggestions |

---

## Workflow Inputs

### Required Inputs

- Skill path (`{skills_output_folder}/{name}/`)

### Optional Inputs

- `--batch` flag with list of skills or directory
- Source path override

---

## Workflow Outputs

### Output Format

- [x] Document-producing
- [ ] Non-document

### Output Files

- Completeness score (pass/fail with percentage)
- Gap report with specific missing items

---

## Agent Integration

### Primary Agent

Ferris (Audit mode)

### Other Agents

None

---

## Implementation Notes

**Use the create-workflow workflow to build this workflow.**

Build priority: Phase 1a (Core Engine)

Supports `--batch` for testing multiple skills.

---

input.content:
# SKF: Skill Forge ‚Äî Evidence-Based Agent Skills Compiler

Automated skill compiler ‚Äî AST-verified, provenance-backed agent skills from source code

Zero hallucination tolerance. Every instruction traces to code.

---

## Overview

Skill Forge transforms source code into agentskills.io-compliant, version-pinned, provenance-backed agent skills. It uses ast-grep for structural truth, QMD for knowledge intelligence, and a progressive capability model (Quick/Forge/Deep) that meets developers where they are.

**Target Users:** Platform Engineers, Library Maintainers, Full-Stack Developers

**The "Aha!" moment:** The first time you see `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` next to an instruction and realize: *"This isn't a summary. This isn't a guess. The tool READ my code and bound this instruction to the exact line."*

---

## Installation

```bash
bmad install skf
```

You'll be prompted for:
- **Skills output folder** ‚Äî Where generated skills are saved (default: `skills/`)
- **Forge data folder** ‚Äî Where workspace artifacts are stored (default: `forge-data/`)

---

## Quick Start

1. **Setup your forge:** `@Ferris SF` ‚Äî detects tools, sets your tier (Quick/Forge/Deep)
2. **Quick skill (fastest):** `@Ferris QS spacetimedb` ‚Äî 47-second skill from a package name
3. **Full skill:** `@Ferris BS` ‚Üí `@Ferris CS` ‚Äî brief then compile for maximum quality
4. **Stack skill:** `@Ferris SS` ‚Äî consolidated project skill with integration patterns
5. **Export:** `@Ferris EX` ‚Äî package for distribution, update CLAUDE.md

**For detailed documentation, see [docs/](docs/).**

---

## Components

### Agents

| Agent | Name | Icon | Role |
|-------|------|------|------|
| Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian | Ferris | ‚öíÔ∏è | Sole agent ‚Äî skill compilation lifecycle |

Ferris operates in 4 workflow-driven modes: Architect, Surgeon, Audit, Delivery.

### Workflows

**Core:**

| Code | Name | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `SF` | Setup Forge | Initialize environment, detect tools, set tier |
| `BS` | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
| `CS` | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief (supports --batch) |
| `US` | Update Skill | Smart regeneration preserving [MANUAL] sections |

**Feature:**

| Code | Name | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `QS` | Quick Skill | Fast skill, no brief needed |
| `SS` | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill |
| `AN` | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill |
| `AS` | Audit Skill | Drift detection |
| `TS` | Test Skill | Completeness verification |

**Utility:**

| Code | Name | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `EX` | Export Skill | Package for distribution |

---

## Configuration

The module supports these configuration options (set during installation):

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `skills_output_folder` | Where generated skills are saved | `{project-root}/skills` |
| `forge_data_folder` | Where workspace artifacts are stored | `{project-root}/forge-data` |

Runtime configuration (tool detection, tier, parallel settings) is managed by the `setup-forge` workflow in `forge-config.yaml`.

---

## Progressive Capability Model

| Tier | Tools | Capability |
|------|-------|------------|
| **Quick** | gh + skills_ref | Source reading + spec validation |
| **Forge** | + ast-grep | Structural truth, T1 confidence |
| **Deep** | + QMD | Knowledge search, temporal provenance |

---

## Module Structure

```
skf/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ module.yaml
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ README.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ TODO.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ getting-started.md
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agents.md
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ workflows.md
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ examples.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agents/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ferris.md
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ workflows/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ setup-forge/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ brief-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ create-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ update-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ quick-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ create-stack-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ analyze-source/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ audit-skill/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-skill/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ export-skill/
```

---

## Documentation

For detailed user guides and documentation, see the **[docs/](docs/)** folder:
- [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md)
- [Agents Reference](docs/agents.md)
- [Workflows Reference](docs/workflows.md)
- [Examples](docs/examples.md)

---

## Development Status

This module is currently in development. The following components are planned:

- [ ] Agents: 1 agent (Ferris)
- [ ] Workflows: 10 workflows

See TODO.md for detailed status.

---

## Author

Created by Armel via BMAD Module workflow

---

## License

Part of the BMAD framework.

input.content:
# Getting Started with SKF: Skill Forge

Welcome to Skill Forge! This guide will help you get up and running.

---

## What This Module Does

Skill Forge is an automated skill compiler for the AI agent ecosystem. It transforms source code into agentskills.io-compliant, version-pinned, provenance-backed agent skills. Every instruction traces to actual code ‚Äî zero hallucination tolerance.

---

## Installation

If you haven't installed the module yet:

```bash
bmad install skf
```

Follow the prompts to configure the module for your needs.

---

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Required For | Install |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `gh` (GitHub CLI) | All modes | https://cli.github.com |
| `ast-grep` | Forge + Deep modes | https://ast-grep.github.io |
| QMD | Deep mode | Already included with BMAD |

Don't worry if you don't have all tools ‚Äî SKF detects what's available and sets your tier automatically.

---

## First Steps

### 1. Setup Your Forge

```
@Ferris SF
```

This detects your tools, sets your capability tier, and initializes the forge environment. You only need to do this once per project.

### 2. Generate Your First Skill

**Fastest path (Quick Skill):**
```
@Ferris QS spacetimedb
```

Ferris resolves the package to GitHub, reads the source, and generates a skill in under a minute.

**Full quality path:**
```
@Ferris BS    # Brief ‚Äî scope and design the skill
@Ferris CS    # Create ‚Äî compile from the brief
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî package for distribution
```

### 3. Stack Skill (for full projects)

```
@Ferris SS
```

Analyzes your project's dependencies and generates a consolidated stack skill with integration patterns.

---

## Common Use Cases

### I need skills for my dependencies
Use Quick Skill (`QS`) for each dependency. It resolves package names to repos automatically.

### I'm onboarding a large existing codebase
Use Analyze Source (`AN`) to discover what to skill, then batch-create with Create Skill (`CS --batch`).

### I maintain an OSS library
Use Brief Skill (`BS`) + Create Skill (`CS`) for maximum quality. Export with `source_authority: official`.

### I want my agent to understand my whole project
Use Stack Skill (`SS`) for a consolidated skill with cross-library integration patterns.

---

## What's Next?

- Check out the [Agents Reference](agents.md) to learn about Ferris
- Browse the [Workflows Reference](workflows.md) to see all available commands
- See [Examples](examples.md) for real-world usage scenarios

---

## Need Help?

If you run into issues:
1. Run `@Ferris SF` to check your tool availability and tier
2. Check `forge-config.yaml` for your current configuration
3. Review the module configuration in your BMAD settings

# Getting Started with SKF: Skill Forge

Welcome to Skill Forge! This guide will help you get up and running.

---

## What This Module Does

Skill Forge is an automated skill compiler for the AI agent ecosystem. It transforms source code into agentskills.io-compliant, version-pinned, provenance-backed agent skills. Every instruction traces to actual code ‚Äî zero hallucination tolerance.

---

## Installation

If you haven't installed the module yet:

```bash
bmad install skf
```

Follow the prompts to configure the module for your needs.

---

## Prerequisites

| Tool | Required For | Install |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `gh` (GitHub CLI) | All modes | https://cli.github.com |
| `ast-grep` | Forge + Deep modes | https://ast-grep.github.io |
| QMD | Deep mode | Already included with BMAD |

Don't worry if you don't have all tools ‚Äî SKF detects what's available and sets your tier automatically.

---

## First Steps

### 1. Setup Your Forge

```
@Ferris SF
```

This detects your tools, sets your capability tier, and initializes the forge environment. You only need to do this once per project.

### 2. Generate Your First Skill

**Fastest path (Quick Skill):**
```
@Ferris QS spacetimedb
```

Ferris resolves the package to GitHub, reads the source, and generates a skill in under a minute.

**Full quality path:**
```
@Ferris BS    # Brief ‚Äî scope and design the skill
@Ferris CS    # Create ‚Äî compile from the brief
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî package for distribution
```

### 3. Stack Skill (for full projects)

```
@Ferris SS
```

Analyzes your project's dependencies and generates a consolidated stack skill with integration patterns.

---

## Common Use Cases

### I need skills for my dependencies
Use Quick Skill (`QS`) for each dependency. It resolves package names to repos automatically.

### I'm onboarding a large existing codebase
Use Analyze Source (`AN`) to discover what to skill, then batch-create with Create Skill (`CS --batch`).

### I maintain an OSS library
Use Brief Skill (`BS`) + Create Skill (`CS`) for maximum quality. Export with `source_authority: official`.

### I want my agent to understand my whole project
Use Stack Skill (`SS`) for a consolidated skill with cross-library integration patterns.

---

## What's Next?

- Check out the [Agents Reference](agents.md) to learn about Ferris
- Browse the [Workflows Reference](workflows.md) to see all available commands
- See [Examples](examples.md) for real-world usage scenarios

---

## Need Help?

If you run into issues:
1. Run `@Ferris SF` to check your tool availability and tier
2. Check `forge-config.yaml` for your current configuration
3. Review the module configuration in your BMAD settings

# Agents Reference

SKF includes 1 specialized agent:

---

## Ferris ‚Äî Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian

**ID:** `_bmad/skf/agents/ferris.md`
**Icon:** ‚öíÔ∏è

**Role:**
Sole agent managing the entire skill compilation lifecycle. Ferris extracts, compiles, validates, and packages agent skills from source code.

**When to Use:**
Ferris handles all SKF workflows. You always interact with Ferris ‚Äî he switches modes based on which workflow you invoke.

**Key Capabilities:**
- AST analysis via ast-grep for structural truth
- QMD knowledge search for temporal context and evidence
- agentskills.io specification compliance and validation
- GitHub source navigation and package-to-repo resolution
- Cross-knowledge synthesis for stack skills and integration patterns

**Workflow-Driven Modes:**

| Mode | Behavior | Workflows |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Architect** | Exploratory, structural, assembling | BS, CS, QS, SS, AN |
| **Surgeon** | Precise, semantic diffing, preserves [MANUAL] | US |
| **Audit** | Judgmental, drift reports, completeness scoring | AS, TS |
| **Delivery** | Packaging, platform-aware, ecosystem-ready | EX |

**Communication Style:**
- During work: structured reports with AST citations, no metaphor
- At transitions: forge language, brief and warm
- On completion: quiet craftsman's pride
- On errors: direct and actionable

**Menu:**

```
‚öíÔ∏è Ferris ‚Äî Skill Forge

START HERE:
  [SF] Setup Forge ‚Äî Initialize your forge environment
  [AN] Analyze Source ‚Äî Discover what to skill

CREATE:
  [BS] Brief Skill ‚Äî Design a skill scope
  [CS] Create Skill ‚Äî Compile a skill from brief
  [QS] Quick Skill ‚Äî Fast skill, no brief needed
  [SS] Stack Skill ‚Äî Consolidated project stack skill

MAINTAIN:
  [US] Update Skill ‚Äî Regenerate after changes
  [AS] Audit Skill ‚Äî Check for drift
  [TS] Test Skill ‚Äî Verify completeness

DELIVER:
  [EX] Export Skill ‚Äî Package for distribution

[WS] Workflow Status
[FM] Full Menu
```

**Memory:**
Ferris has a sidecar (`_bmad/_memory/ferris-sidecar/`) that persists user preferences and tool availability across sessions.

input.content:
# Workflows Reference

SKF includes 10 workflows organized by purpose:

---

## Core Workflows

### Setup Forge (SF)

**Command:** `@Ferris SF`

**Purpose:** Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set capability tier, auto-index project.

**When to Use:** First time using SKF in a project. Run once per project.

**Key Steps:** Detect tools ‚Üí Determine tier ‚Üí Create forge-config.yaml ‚Üí Auto-index (Deep)

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Brief Skill (BS)

**Command:** `@Ferris BS`

**Purpose:** Scope and design a skill through guided discovery.

**When to Use:** Before `Create Skill` when you want maximum control over what gets compiled.

**Key Steps:** Gather intent ‚Üí Analyze target ‚Üí Define scope ‚Üí Write skill-brief.yaml

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Create Skill (CS)

**Command:** `@Ferris CS`

**Purpose:** Compile a skill from a brief. Supports `--batch` for multiple briefs.

**When to Use:** After Brief Skill, or with an existing skill-brief.yaml.

**Key Steps:** Load brief ‚Üí Ecosystem check ‚Üí AST extract ‚Üí QMD enrich ‚Üí Compile ‚Üí Validate ‚Üí Generate

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Update Skill (US)

**Command:** `@Ferris US`

**Purpose:** Smart regeneration preserving `[MANUAL]` sections. Detects individual vs stack internally.

**When to Use:** After source code changes when an existing skill needs updating.

**Key Steps:** Load existing ‚Üí Detect changes ‚Üí Re-extract ‚Üí Merge (preserve MANUAL) ‚Üí Validate ‚Üí Write

**Agent:** Ferris (Surgeon mode)

---

## Feature Workflows

### Quick Skill (QS)

**Command:** `@Ferris QS <package-or-url>`

**Purpose:** Brief-less fast skill with package-to-repo resolution.

**When to Use:** When you need a skill quickly ‚Äî no brief needed. Accepts package names or GitHub URLs.

**Key Steps:** Resolve target ‚Üí Ecosystem check ‚Üí Quick extract ‚Üí Compile ‚Üí Validate ‚Üí Write

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Stack Skill (SS)

**Command:** `@Ferris SS`

**Purpose:** Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns.

**When to Use:** When you want your agent to understand your entire project stack ‚Äî not just individual libraries.

**Key Steps:** Detect manifests ‚Üí Rank dependencies ‚Üí Scope confirmation ‚Üí Parallel extract ‚Üí Detect integrations ‚Üí Compile stack ‚Üí Generate references

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Analyze Source (AN)

**Command:** `@Ferris AN`

**Purpose:** Decomposition engine ‚Äî discover what to skill, recommend stack skill.

**When to Use:** Brownfield onboarding of large repos or multi-service projects.

**Key Steps:** Scan project ‚Üí Identify units ‚Üí Map exports ‚Üí Detect integrations ‚Üí Recommend ‚Üí Generate briefs

**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)

---

### Audit Skill (AS)

**Command:** `@Ferris AS`

**Purpose:** Drift detection between skill and current source.

**When to Use:** To check if a skill has fallen out of date with its source code.

**Key Steps:** Load skill ‚Üí Re-index source ‚Üí Structural diff ‚Üí Semantic diff (Deep) ‚Üí Classify severity ‚Üí Report

**Agent:** Ferris (Audit mode)

---

### Test Skill (TS)

**Command:** `@Ferris TS`

**Purpose:** Cognitive completeness verification. Quality gate before export.

**When to Use:** After creating or updating a skill, before exporting.

**Key Steps:** Load skill ‚Üí Detect mode ‚Üí Coverage check ‚Üí Coherence check ‚Üí Score ‚Üí Gap report

**Agent:** Ferris (Audit mode)

---

## Utility Workflows

### Export Skill (EX)

**Command:** `@Ferris EX`

**Purpose:** Package for distribution + platform-aware context injection into CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.

**When to Use:** When a skill is ready for distribution or CLAUDE.md integration.

**Key Steps:** Load skill ‚Üí Package ‚Üí Generate snippet ‚Üí Update CLAUDE.md ‚Üí Token report ‚Üí Summary

**Agent:** Ferris (Delivery mode)

---

## Workflow Connections

```
                    setup-forge (one-time)
                         ‚îÇ
          ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
          ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
     analyze-source  quick-skill  create-stack-skill
          ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
     brief-skill         ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
          ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
     create-skill        ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
          ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ              ‚îÇ
          ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
                 ‚îÇ
            test-skill (quality gate)
                 ‚îÇ
          ‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
          ‚îÇ             ‚îÇ
     export-skill  audit-skill
                        ‚îÇ
                   update-skill (loops back to test)
```

---

## Build Priority

| Phase | Workflows | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| 1a (Core Engine) | setup-forge, create-skill, test-skill, export-skill | Must build first |
| 1b (Entry Points) | quick-skill, brief-skill, create-stack-skill | Complete v1.0 |
| 2 (Lifecycle) | audit-skill, update-skill, analyze-source | Post-launch |

input.content:
# Examples & Use Cases

This section provides practical examples for using SKF: Skill Forge.

---

## Example Workflows

### Quick Skill ‚Äî 47 Seconds

Developer adds `spacetimedb` to a Next.js project. Agent keeps hallucinating methods.

```
@Ferris QS spacetimedb
```

Ferris resolves to GitHub, extracts 14 public functions via source reading, validates against spec. Skill appears in `skills/spacetimedb/`. Agent stops hallucinating. Forty-seven seconds. Done.

### Brownfield Platform ‚Äî 8 Minutes

Alex's team adopts BMAD for 10 microservices (TypeScript, Go, Rust).

```
@Ferris SF          # Setup ‚Äî Deep mode detected
@Ferris AN          # Analyze ‚Äî 10 services mapped
@Ferris CS --batch  # Create ‚Äî batch generation
```

10 individual skills + 1 platform stack skill. BMM architect navigates cross-service flows with verified knowledge.

### Release Prep ‚Äî Trust Builder

Sarah prepares v3.0.0 with breaking changes.

```
@Ferris AS    # Audit ‚Äî finds 3 renames, 1 removal, 1 addition
@Ferris US    # Update ‚Äî preserves [MANUAL] sections, adds annotations
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî package for npm release
```

Ships with npm release. Consumers upgrade ‚Äî their agents use the correct function names. Zero hallucination tickets.

### Stack Skill ‚Äî Integration Intelligence

Armel's full-stack project: Next.js + Serwist + SpacetimeDB + better-auth.

```
@Ferris SS
```

Ferris detects 8 significant dependencies, finds 5 co-import integration points. Generates a consolidated stack skill. The agent now knows: "When you modify the auth flow, update the Serwist cache exclusion at `src/sw.ts:L23`." Integration intelligence no other tool provides.

---

## Common Scenarios

### Scenario A: Greenfield + BMM Integration

BMAD user starts a new project. BMM architect suggests skill generation after retrospective.

```
@Ferris BS    # Brief ‚Äî scope the skill
@Ferris CS    # Create ‚Äî compile from brief
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî inject into CLAUDE.md
```

Skills accumulate over sprints. Agent gets smarter every iteration.

### Scenario B: Multi-Repo Platform

Alex needs cross-service knowledge for 10 microservices.

One forge project, multiple QMD collections, hub-and-spoke skills with integration patterns.

### Scenario C: External Dependency

Developer needs skills for a library that doesn't have official skills.

```
@Ferris QS better-auth
```

Checks ecosystem first. If no official skill exists: generates from source. `source_authority: community`.

### Scenario D: Docs-Only (SaaS/Closed Source)

No source code available ‚Äî only documentation.

Generate from docs + QMD-indexed content. T2/T3 confidence only. `source_authority: community`.

---

## Tips & Tricks

### Progressive Capability

Start with Quick mode (no setup required), upgrade to Forge (install ast-grep), then Deep (QMD already included). Each tier builds on the previous ‚Äî you never lose capability.

### Batch Operations

Use `--batch` with `create-skill` and `test-skill` to process multiple skills at once. Progress is checkpointed ‚Äî use `--continue` to resume if interrupted.

### Stack Skills + Individual Skills

Stack skills focus on integration patterns. Individual skills focus on API surface. Use both together for maximum coverage.

### The Loop

After each sprint's refactor, run `@Ferris US` to regenerate changed components. Export updates CLAUDE.md automatically. Skill generation becomes routine ‚Äî like running tests.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

**"Forge halted: ast-grep not found"**
Install ast-grep to unlock Forge mode: https://ast-grep.github.io

**"No brief found"**
Run `@Ferris BS` first to create a skill brief, or use `@Ferris QS` for brief-less generation.

**"Ecosystem check: official skill exists"**
An official skill already exists for this package. Consider installing it with `npx skills add` instead of generating your own.

**Quick mode skills have lower confidence**
Quick mode reads source without AST analysis. Install ast-grep to upgrade to Forge mode for structural truth (T1 confidence).

---

## Getting More Help

- Run `@Ferris SF` to check your current tier and tool availability
- Review `forge-config.yaml` for runtime configuration
- Check module configuration in your BMAD settings
- Consult the broader BMAD documentation

@Ferris AN          # Analyze ‚Äî 10 services mapped
@Ferris CS --batch  # Create ‚Äî batch generation
```

10 individual skills + 1 platform stack skill. BMM architect navigates cross-service flows with verified knowledge.

### Release Prep ‚Äî Trust Builder

Sarah prepares v3.0.0 with breaking changes.

```
@Ferris AS    # Audit ‚Äî finds 3 renames, 1 removal, 1 addition
@Ferris US    # Update ‚Äî preserves [MANUAL] sections, adds annotations
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî package for npm release
```

Ships with npm release. Consumers upgrade ‚Äî their agents use the correct function names. Zero hallucination tickets.

### Stack Skill ‚Äî Integration Intelligence

Armel's full-stack project: Next.js + Serwist + SpacetimeDB + better-auth.

```
@Ferris SS
```

Ferris detects 8 significant dependencies, finds 5 co-import integration points. Generates a consolidated stack skill. The agent now knows: "When you modify the auth flow, update the Serwist cache exclusion at `src/sw.ts:L23`." Integration intelligence no other tool provides.

---

## Common Scenarios

### Scenario A: Greenfield + BMM Integration

BMAD user starts a new project. BMM architect suggests skill generation after retrospective.

```
@Ferris BS    # Brief ‚Äî scope the skill
@Ferris CS    # Create ‚Äî compile from brief
@Ferris TS    # Test ‚Äî verify completeness
@Ferris EX    # Export ‚Äî inject into CLAUDE.md
```

Skills accumulate over sprints. Agent gets smarter every iteration.

### Scenario B: Multi-Repo Platform

Alex needs cross-service knowledge for 10 microservices.

One forge project, multiple QMD collections, hub-and-spoke skills with integration patterns.

### Scenario C: External Dependency

Developer needs skills for a library that doesn't have official skills.

```
@Ferris QS better-auth
```

Checks ecosystem first. If no official skill exists: generates from source. `source_authority: community`.

### Scenario D: Docs-Only (SaaS/Closed Source)

No source code available ‚Äî only documentation.

Generate from docs + QMD-indexed content. T2/T3 confidence only. `source_authority: community`.

---

## Tips & Tricks

### Progressive Capability

Start with Quick mode (no setup required), upgrade to Forge (install ast-grep), then Deep (QMD already included). Each tier builds on the previous ‚Äî you never lose capability.

### Batch Operations

Use `--batch` with `create-skill` and `test-skill` to process multiple skills at once. Progress is checkpointed ‚Äî use `--continue` to resume if interrupted.

### Stack Skills + Individual Skills

Stack skills focus on integration patterns. Individual skills focus on API surface. Use both together for maximum coverage.

### The Loop

After each sprint's refactor, run `@Ferris US` to regenerate changed components. Export updates CLAUDE.md automatically. Skill generation becomes routine ‚Äî like running tests.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

**"Forge halted: ast-grep not found"**
Install ast-grep to unlock Forge mode: https://ast-grep.github.io

**"No brief found"**
Run `@Ferris BS` first to create a skill brief, or use `@Ferris QS` for brief-less generation.

**"Ecosystem check: official skill exists"**
An official skill already exists for this package. Consider installing it with `npx skills add` instead of generating your own.

**Quick mode skills have lower confidence**
Quick mode reads source without AST analysis. Install ast-grep to upgrade to Forge mode for structural truth (T1 confidence).

---

## Getting More Help

- Run `@Ferris SF` to check your current tier and tool availability
- Review `forge-config.yaml` for runtime configuration
- Check module configuration in your BMAD settings
- Consult the broader BMAD documentation

---
name: 'step-07-complete'
description: 'Finalize, offer to run validation'

buildTrackingFile: '{bmb_creations_output_folder}/modules/module-build-{module_code}.md'
targetLocation: '{build_tracking_targetLocation}'
moduleHelpGenerateWorkflow: '../module-help-generate.md'
validationWorkflow: '../steps-v/step-01-validate.md'
moduleHelpCsvFile: '{build_tracking_targetLocation}/module-help.csv'
---

# Step 7: Complete

## STEP GOAL:

Finalize the module build, update tracking, and offer to run validation.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES:

### Universal Rules:

- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- ‚úÖ Speak in `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:

- ‚úÖ You are the **Module Builder** ‚Äî completing the build
- ‚úÖ Celebrate what was created
- ‚úÖ Guide next steps

---

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Generate module-help.csv

"**üéØ Generating module-help.csv...**"

Load and execute the module-help-generate workflow:
```
{moduleHelpGenerateWorkflow}
```

**Set these variables before loading:**
- `modulePath: {targetLocation}`
- `moduleYamlFile: {targetLocation}/module.yaml`
- `moduleHelpCsvFile: {targetLocation}/module-help.csv`
- `workflowsDir: {targetLocation}/workflows`
- `agentsDir: {targetLocation}/agents`

**What this does:**
- Scans all workflows in `{workflowsDir}/`
- Scans all agents in `{agentsDir}/`
- Generates `{moduleHelpCsvFile}` with proper structure:
  - `anytime` entries at top (no sequence)
  - Phased entries below (phase-1, phase-2, etc.)
  - Agent-only entries have empty `workflow-file`

**Wait for workflow completion** before proceeding.

### 2. Final Build Summary

"**üéâ Module structure build complete!**"

**Module:** {moduleName} ({moduleCode})
**Type:** {moduleType}
**Location:** {targetLocation}

**What was created:**

| Component | Count | Location |
|-----------|-------|----------|
| Agent specs | {count} | agents/ |
| Workflow specs | {count} | workflows/ |
| Configuration | 1 | module.yaml |
| Help Registry | 1 | module-help.csv |
| Documentation | 2 | README.md, TODO.md |

### 3. Update Build Tracking

Update `{buildTrackingFile}`:
```yaml
---
moduleCode: {module_code}
moduleName: {name}
moduleType: {type}
targetLocation: {location}
stepsCompleted: ['step-01-load-brief', 'step-02-structure', 'step-03-config', 'step-04-agents', 'step-05-workflows', 'step-06-docs', 'step-07-complete']
created: {created_date}
completed: {date}
status: COMPLETE
---
```

### 3. Next Steps

"**Your module structure is ready! Here's what to do next:**"

1. **Review the build** ‚Äî Check {targetLocation}
2. **Build agents** ‚Äî Use `bmad:bmb:agents:agent-builder` for each agent spec
3. **Build workflows** ‚Äî Use `bmad:bmb:workflows:workflow` for each workflow spec
4. **Test installation** ‚Äî Run `bmad install {module_code}`
5. **Iterate** ‚Äî Refine based on testing

### 4. Offer Validation

"**Would you like to run validation on the module structure?**"

Validation checks:
- File structure compliance
- module.yaml correctness
- Spec completeness
- Installation readiness

### 5. MENU OPTIONS

**Select an Option:** [V] Validate Module [D] Done

#### EXECUTION RULES:

- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input

#### Menu Handling Logic:

- IF V: Load `{validationWorkflow}` to run validation
- IF D: Celebration message, workflow complete
- IF Any other: Help user, then redisplay menu

### 6. Completion Message (if Done selected)

"**üöÄ You've built a module structure for BMAD!**"

"**Module:** {moduleName} ({moduleCode})"
"**Location:** {targetLocation}"
"**Status:** Ready for agent and workflow implementation"

"**The journey from idea to installable module continues:**
- Agent specs ‚Üí create-agent workflow
- Workflow specs ‚Üí create-workflow workflow
- Full module ‚Üí `bmad install`

"**Great work! Let's build something amazing.** ‚ú®"

---

## Success Metrics

‚úÖ module-help.csv generated at module root
‚úÖ Build tracking marked COMPLETE
‚úÖ Summary presented to user
‚úÖ Next steps clearly explained
‚úÖ Validation offered (optional)

content.content:
1‚Üí---
     2‚Üíname: module-help-generate
     3‚Üídescription: Generate or update module-help.csv for any BMad module with proper structure
     4‚Üíweb_bundle: false
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí# Path variables (to be set by caller)
     7‚ÜímodulePath: '{module_path}'
     8‚ÜímoduleYamlFile: '{module_path}/module.yaml'
     9‚ÜímoduleHelpCsvFile: '{module_path}/module-help.csv'
    10‚ÜíworkflowsDir: '{module_path}/workflows'
    11‚ÜíagentsDir: '{module_path}/agents'
    12‚Üí---
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí# Module Help CSV Generator
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí**Goal:** Generate or update a `module-help.csv` file that serves as the central registry for all module functionality - workflows, agents, and commands.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí**Your Role:** You are a Module Documentation Architect. You will analyze a module's structure and create a properly formatted CSV that enables discoverability and CLI integration.
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí---
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí## CRITICAL RULES (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí- ALWAYS read existing `module-help.csv` first if it exists - update/validate rather than replace
    25‚Üí- ALWAYS read `module.yaml` to get module code and type
    26‚Üí- ALWAYS read ALL agent `.yaml` files to understand menu triggers
    27‚Üí- ALWAYS read ALL workflow `workflow.md` files to understand purpose
    28‚Üí- ALWAYS place `anytime` entries at the TOP with EMPTY sequence
    29‚Üí- ALWAYS place phased entries BELOW anytime entries
    30‚Üí- ALWAYS number phases starting at `-1` (phase-1, phase-2, phase-3...)
    31‚Üí- ALWAYS leave sequence EMPTY for `anytime` entries (user chooses, not ordered)
    32‚Üí- ALWAYS include sequence number for phased entries (defines order within phase)
    33‚Üí- ALWAYS use EMPTY `workflow-file` for agent-only menu triggers
    34‚Üí- ALWAYS include `agent` column for agent-based features
    35‚Üí- NEVER assume workflow paths - verify from actual file structure
    36‚Üí- ALWAYS search for and put the file at the root of the module ONLY
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí---
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí## CSV STRUCTURE (13 columns)
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí```
    43‚Üímodule,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
    44‚Üí```
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí| Column | Purpose | Rules |
    47‚Üí|--------|---------|-------|
    48‚Üí| `module` | Module code from `module.yaml` | Required |
    49‚Üí| `phase` | `anytime` or `phase-1`, `phase-2`, etc. | Phases start at -1 |
    50‚Üí| `name` | Display name of the feature | User-facing |
    51‚Üí| `code` | Short code for commands | Unique within module |
    52‚Üí| `sequence` | Order within phase | EMPTY for anytime, number for phases |
    53‚Üí| `workflow-file` | Path to workflow.md | EMPTY for agent-only |
    54‚Üí| `command` | Internal command name | Format: `{module_code}_{feature_code}` |
    55‚Üí| `required` | Whether required | Usually `false` |
    56‚Üí| `agent` | Associated agent name | From agent YAML metadata |
    57‚Üí| `options` | Mode or action type | e.g., "Create Mode", "Chat Mode" |
    58‚Üí| `description` | User-facing description | Explain what and when to use |
    59‚Üí| `output-location` | Where output goes | Folder name or EMPTY |
    60‚Üí| `outputs` | What is produced | Output type or EMPTY |
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí---
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí## PHASE AND SEQUENCING RULES
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí### 1. anytime
    67‚Üí- Use for: standalone features, agent menu triggers, unrelated utilities
    68‚Üí- Place at TOP of file
    69‚Üí- `sequence` column MUST BE EMPTY
    70‚Üí- User chooses what to run - no order
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí### 2. Phases (phase-1, phase-2, phase-3...)
    73‚Üí- Use for: sequential workflows, guided processes
    74‚Üí- Place BELOW anytime entries
    75‚Üí- Phases ALWAYS start at `-1` (not 0 or 1)
    76‚Üí- `sequence` defines order WITHIN phase (10, 20, 30...)
    77‚Üí- the name can be named differently than just phase but should be dash number at the end if sequence is needed
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí### 3. Module Integration Patterns
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí**Full module with phases:**
    82‚Üí```
    83‚Üíanytime entries (sequence empty)
    84‚Üíphase-1 entries (sequence 10, 20, 30...)
    85‚Üíphase-2 entries (sequence 10, 20, 30...)
    86‚Üí```
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí**Add-on to existing module:**
    89‚Üí```
    90‚ÜíMay only have phase-3 entries that integrate into another module's workflow
    91‚ÜíSequence numbers fit logically before/after existing items
    92‚Üí```
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí**Standalone collections:**
    95‚Üí```
    96‚ÜíAll entries are anytime
    97‚ÜíNo sequence numbers
    98‚ÜíUser picks one as needed
    99‚Üí```
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí**Agent-only features:**
   102‚Üí```
   103‚ÜíEmpty workflow-file column
   104‚ÜíAgent handles everything via its menu
   105‚Üí```
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí---
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí## EXECUTION SEQUENCE
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí### Step 1: Identify Target Module
   112‚Üí
   113‚ÜíAsk user:
   114‚Üí1. What is the path to the module?
   115‚Üí2. Or should we scan for modules in the workspace?
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí### Step 2: Read Module Configuration
   118‚Üí
   119‚ÜíLoad and read:
   120‚Üí```
   121‚Üí{moduleYamlFile}
   122‚Üí```
   123‚Üí
   124‚ÜíExtract:
   125‚Üí- `code` - Module identifier
   126‚Üí- `type` - Module type
   127‚Üí- `name` - Module display name
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí### Step 3: Check for Existing module-help.csv
   130‚Üí
   131‚ÜíCheck if exists:
   132‚Üí```
   133‚Üí{moduleHelpCsvFile}
   134‚Üí```
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí**If exists:**
   137‚Üí- Read entire file
   138‚Üí- Parse all existing entries
   139‚Üí- Ask user: Update existing, validate, or regenerate?
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí**If not exists:**
   142‚Üí- Note: Will create new file
   143‚Üí- Proceed to discovery
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí### Step 4: Discover All Workflows
   146‚Üí
   147‚ÜíScan the workflows directory:
   148‚Üí```
   149‚Üí{workflowsDir}
   150‚Üí```
   151‚Üí
   152‚ÜíFor each workflow found:
   153‚Üí- Read the `workflow.md` file
   154‚Üí- Extract: name, description, goal, role
   155‚Üí- Note the relative path for CSV entry
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí### Step 5: Discover All Agents
   158‚Üí
   159‚ÜíScan the agents directory:
   160‚Üí```

file.content:
---
name: module-help-generate
description: Generate or update module-help.csv for any BMad module with proper structure
web_bundle: false

# Path variables (to be set by caller)
modulePath: '{module_path}'
moduleYamlFile: '{module_path}/module.yaml'
moduleHelpCsvFile: '{module_path}/module-help.csv'
workflowsDir: '{module_path}/workflows'
agentsDir: '{module_path}/agents'
---

# Module Help CSV Generator

**Goal:** Generate or update a `module-help.csv` file that serves as the central registry for all module functionality - workflows, agents, and commands.

**Your Role:** You are a Module Documentation Architect. You will analyze a module's structure and create a properly formatted CSV that enables discoverability and CLI integration.

---

## CRITICAL RULES (NO EXCEPTIONS)

- ALWAYS read existing `module-help.csv` first if it exists - update/validate rather than replace
- ALWAYS read `module.yaml` to get module code and type
- ALWAYS read ALL agent `.yaml` files to understand menu triggers
- ALWAYS read ALL workflow `workflow.md` files to understand purpose
- ALWAYS place `anytime` entries at the TOP with EMPTY sequence
- ALWAYS place phased entries BELOW anytime entries
- ALWAYS number phases starting at `-1` (phase-1, phase-2, phase-3...)
- ALWAYS leave sequence EMPTY for `anytime` entries (user chooses, not ordered)
- ALWAYS include sequence number for phased entries (defines order within phase)
- ALWAYS use EMPTY `workflow-file` for agent-only menu triggers
- ALWAYS include `agent` column for agent-based features
- NEVER assume workflow paths - verify from actual file structure
- ALWAYS search for and put the file at the root of the module ONLY

---

## CSV STRUCTURE (13 columns)

```
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
```

| Column | Purpose | Rules |
|--------|---------|-------|
| `module` | Module code from `module.yaml` | Required |
| `phase` | `anytime` or `phase-1`, `phase-2`, etc. | Phases start at -1 |
| `name` | Display name of the feature | User-facing |
| `code` | Short code for commands | Unique within module |
| `sequence` | Order within phase | EMPTY for anytime, number for phases |
| `workflow-file` | Path to workflow.md | EMPTY for agent-only |
| `command` | Internal command name | Format: `{module_code}_{feature_code}` |
| `required` | Whether required | Usually `false` |
| `agent` | Associated agent name | From agent YAML metadata |
| `options` | Mode or action type | e.g., "Create Mode", "Chat Mode" |
| `description` | User-facing description | Explain what and when to use |
| `output-location` | Where output goes | Folder name or EMPTY |
| `outputs` | What is produced | Output type or EMPTY |

---

## PHASE AND SEQUENCING RULES

### 1. anytime
- Use for: standalone features, agent menu triggers, unrelated utilities
- Place at TOP of file
- `sequence` column MUST BE EMPTY
- User chooses what to run - no order

### 2. Phases (phase-1, phase-2, phase-3...)
- Use for: sequential workflows, guided processes
- Place BELOW anytime entries
- Phases ALWAYS start at `-1` (not 0 or 1)
- `sequence` defines order WITHIN phase (10, 20, 30...)
- the name can be named differently than just phase but should be dash number at the end if sequence is needed

### 3. Module Integration Patterns

**Full module with phases:**
```
anytime entries (sequence empty)
phase-1 entries (sequence 10, 20, 30...)
phase-2 entries (sequence 10, 20, 30...)
```

**Add-on to existing module:**
```
May only have phase-3 entries that integrate into another module's workflow
Sequence numbers fit logically before/after existing items
```

**Standalone collections:**
```
All entries are anytime
No sequence numbers
User picks one as needed
```

**Agent-only features:**
```
Empty workflow-file column
Agent handles everything via its menu
```

---

## EXECUTION SEQUENCE

### Step 1: Identify Target Module

Ask user:
1. What is the path to the module?
2. Or should we scan for modules in the workspace?

### Step 2: Read Module Configuration

Load and read:
```
{moduleYamlFile}
```

Extract:
- `code` - Module identifier
- `type` - Module type
- `name` - Module display name

### Step 3: Check for Existing module-help.csv

Check if exists:
```
{moduleHelpCsvFile}
```

**If exists:**
- Read entire file
- Parse all existing entries
- Ask user: Update existing, validate, or regenerate?

**If not exists:**
- Note: Will create new file
- Proceed to discovery

### Step 4: Discover All Workflows

Scan the workflows directory:
```
{workflowsDir}
```

For each workflow found:
- Read the `workflow.md` file
- Extract: name, description, goal, role
- Note the relative path for CSV entry

### Step 5: Discover All Agents

Scan the agents directory:
```
{agentsDir}
```

For each agent found:
- Read the `.agent.yaml` file
- Extract: metadata (name, title), persona, menu triggers
- Identify agent-only triggers (no workflow route)
- Identify workflow-routing triggers

### Step 6: Determine Phasing Strategy

Analyze the module and decide:

**Question for each workflow:**
- Is this part of a sequential journey? ‚Üí Use phases
- Is this standalone/optional? ‚Üí Use anytime
- Can user do this anytime? ‚Üí Use anytime

**For agent menu items:**
- Does it route to a workflow? ‚Üí Map to that workflow or anytime
- Is it an inline action? ‚Üí anytime, no workflow file

### Step 7: Generate CSV Content

Build the CSV following structure:

**Header:**
```
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
```

**Entry Rules:**
1. ALL `anytime` entries FIRST - `sequence` EMPTY
2. THEN phased entries - `phase-1`, `phase-2`, etc.
3. Within phases, `sequence` orders execution (10, 20, 30...)
4. Agent-only actions: empty `workflow-file`, specify `agent`

**Code Format:**
- Command: `{module_code}_{feature_name}`
- Keep codes short but memorable (2-3 letters usually)

**Description Guidance:**
- Explain WHAT the feature does
- Include WHEN to use it (especially for phased items)
- For add-on modules: "Best used after X but before Y"

### Step 8: Present to User

Before writing:
1. Show the CSV content in a readable table format
2. Explain phasing decisions
3. Highlight any agent-only entries
4. Ask for confirmation or adjustments

### Step 9: Write File

On confirmation:
```
Write to: {moduleHelpCsvFile}
```

---

## EXAMPLE OUTPUT STRUCTURE

### Full Module with Phases (like mwm):
```csv
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
mwm,anytime,Chat with Wellness,CWC,,"mwm_chat",false,wellness-companion,Chat Mode,"Have a supportive conversation anytime",,,
mwm,anytime,Quick Breathing,QB,,"mwm_breathing",false,meditation-guide,Breathing,"Quick 4-7-8 breathing exercise",,,
mwm,phase-1,Daily Check In,DCI,10,_bmad/mwm/workflows/daily-checkin/workflow.md,mwm_daily_checkin,false,wellness-companion,Check In Mode,"Start your day with wellness check-in",mwm_output,"summary",
mwm,phase-2,Wellness Journal,WJ,20,_bmad/mwm/workflows/wellness-journal/workflow.md,mwm_journal,false,wellness-companion,Journal Mode,"Reflect and track your wellness journey",mwm_output,"entry",
```

### Standalone Module (like bmad-custom):
```csv
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
bmad-custom,anytime,Quiz Master,QM,,"bmad_quiz",false,,Trivia,"Interactive trivia quiz with gameshow atmosphere",bmad_output,"results",
bmad-custom,anytime,Wassup,WS,,"bmad_wassup",false,,Status,"Check uncommitted changes and suggest commits",bmad_output,"summary",
bmad-custom,anytime,Write Commit,WC,,"bmad_write_commit",false,commit-poet,Write,"Craft a commit message from your changes",bmad_output,"message",
```

---

## INITIALIZATION

To begin this workflow:

1. Ask user for the target module path if not provided
2. Load and read `module.yaml` in the root of the target if it exists
3. Check for existing `module-help.csv`
4. Scan for all workflows and agents
5. Generate CSV following all rules above
6. Update the file and review with the user - never auto commit and push

content.thinking:
Now I need to generate the module-help.csv. Based on the brief, SKF has:
- 10 workflows, all owned by Ferris
- All workflows are standalone/anytime ‚Äî the user can invoke any command at any time through Ferris's menu
- However, there IS a logical ordering: setup-forge should come first, then create flows, then maintain, then deliver
- But looking at how the brief describes it ‚Äî Ferris enforces prerequisites via redirects, not via phasing. The menu is grouped by purpose (START HERE, CREATE, MAINTAIN, DELIVER) but users can try any command anytime.

So the phasing strategy:


- All workflows should be `anytime` since Ferris handles prerequisites internally
- Or we could use phases to represent the logical groups: start-1, create-2, maintain-3, deliver-4

Looking at the CIS example with brainstorming/storytelling/etc ‚Äî those are all anytime. Looking at BMM which has phases (1-discover, 2-define, 3-build, 4-ship).

For SKF, the brief says Ferris enforces a dependency graph through redirects, never errors, and users invoke workflows through menu codes. The menu is grouped but any command can be tried anytime. I think the best approach is to use phases that mirror the menu groupings ‚Äî this gives structure without blocking users, since Ferris will handle any invalid sequences gracefully. I need to verify the actual module-help.csv format by checking what's already there, since the brief emphasizes that Ferris handles prerequisites through redirects‚Äîmeaning all workflows should be marked as `anytime`. I'll generate the CSV entries following the established pattern, with workflow paths pointing to the SKF module's workflow files.

# module-help.csv - Workflow Menu Configuration - REMOVE THESE COMMENTS
# This example file defines all workflows in your module for the BMAD Help system
#
# Columns:
# - module: Your module code (from module.yaml)
# - phase: 'anytime' OR numbered phases `1-discover 2-define 3-build 4-ship` if sequential or named phases without a number if the sequence only matters within a phase but the phase order itself is stand alone or does not matter.
# - name: Display name of the workflow
# - code: 2-letter menu code (used for quick commands like /module:code:workflow-name)
# - sequence: Order within phase (10, 20, 30...) IF ORDER MATTERS - for anytime or ordering not mattering, do not give it a sequence number
# - workflow-file: Path to workflow.yaml or workflow.md
# - command: Full command syntax (bmad:module-code:workflow-name)
# - required: true/false - whether this workflow must complete before later phases
# - agent: Agent name from agents/*.agent.yaml files
# - options: Additional workflow options (e.g., "Create Mode")
# - description: Brief description of what the workflow does
# - output-location: Variable name from module.yaml for where outputs go
# - outputs: Expected output artifact names
#
# IMPORTANT: Empty phase = anytime (universal tools work regardless of workflow state)
#
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,

# module-help.csv - Workflow Menu Configuration - REMOVE THESE COMMENTS
# This example file defines all workflows in your module for the BMAD Help system
#
# Columns:
# - module: Your module code (from module.yaml)
# - phase: 'anytime' OR numbered phases `1-discover 2-define 3-build 4-ship` if sequential or named phases without a number if the sequence only matters within a phase but the phase order itself is stand alone or does not matter.
# - name: Display name of the workflow
# - code: 2-letter menu code (used for quick commands like /module:code:workflow-name)
# - sequence: Order within phase (10, 20, 30...) IF ORDER MATTERS - for anytime or ordering not mattering, do not give it a sequence number
# - workflow-file: Path to workflow.yaml or workflow.md
# - command: Full command syntax (bmad:module-code:workflow-name)
# - required: true/false - whether this workflow must complete before later phases
# - agent: Agent name from agents/*.agent.yaml files
# - options: Additional workflow options (e.g., "Create Mode")
# - description: Brief description of what the workflow does
# - output-location: Variable name from module.yaml for where outputs go
# - outputs: Expected output artifact names
#
# IMPORTANT: Empty phase = anytime (universal tools work regardless of workflow state)
#
module,phase,name,code,sequence,workflow-file,command,required,agent,options,description,output-location,outputs,
# anytime workflows - available at any time

# numbered phases - sequential workflow stages
code,1-discover,Discovery Step,DS,10,_bmad/code/workflows/discovery/workflow.yaml,bmad-code-discovery,false,example-agent,Create Mode,"First phase of sequential workflow",output_folder,"discovery document",

code,3-build,Build Step,BS,10,_bmad/code/workflows/build/workflow.yaml,bmad-code-build,true,example-agent,Create Mode,"Build and implement phase",output_folder,"implementation",
code,4-ship,Ship Step,SS,10,_bmad/code/workflows/ship/workflow.yaml,bmad-code-ship,false,example-agent,Create Mode,"Final delivery and release phase",output_folder,"release",

# Step 1: Load Target (Validate Mode)

## STEP GOAL:

Load the target (brief, module, agent specs, or workflow specs) for validation.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES:

### Universal Rules:
- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- ‚úÖ Speak in `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:
- ‚úÖ You are the **Quality Assurance** ‚Äî thorough, systematic
- ‚úÖ Understand what we're validating

---

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Determine Validation Target

"**What would you like to validate?**"

Options:
- **[B]rief** ‚Äî Module brief from Brief mode
- **[M]odule** ‚Äî Built module structure
- **[A]gents** ‚Äî Agent specifications
- **[W]orkflows** ‚Äî Workflow specifications
- **[F]ull** ‚Äî Everything (brief + module + specs)

### 2. Load Target

Based on selection, load the target:

**IF Brief:**
- Path: `{bmb_creations_output_folder}/modules/module-brief-{code}.md`
- Ask for module code if not specified

**IF Module:**
- Path: `src/modules/{code}/`
- Ask for module code if not specified

**IF Agents:**
- Path: `src/modules/{code}/agents/`
- Load all `.spec.md` or `.agent.yaml` files

**IF Workflows:**
- Path: `src/modules/{code}/workflows/`
- Load all `.spec.md` files

**IF Full:**
- Load everything above for a module

### 3. Confirm Target

"**Validating:** {target_type} for {module_code}"
"**Location:** {path}"

"**Shall I proceed?**"

### 4. Initialize Validation Report

Create the validation report structure:

```yaml
---
validationDate: {timestamp}
targetType: {target_type}
moduleCode: {module_code}
targetPath: {path}
status: IN_PROGRESS
---
```

### 5. Proceed to Validation

"**Starting validation checks...**"

Load `{nextStepFile}` to begin file structure validation.

---

## Success Metrics

‚úÖ Validation report initialized
‚úÖ User confirmed

targetPath: '{validation_target_path}'
---

# Step 3: module.yaml Validation

## STEP GOAL:

Validate module.yaml formatting and conventions.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES:

### Universal Rules:
- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- ‚úÖ Speak in `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:
- ‚úÖ You are the **Quality Assurance** ‚Äî checking configuration
- ‚úÖ Ensure proper YAML syntax

---

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Load module.yaml

Read `{targetPath}/module.yaml`

**IF not present:**
- Record as FAIL (required file)
- Skip to next validation

### 2. Validate Required Fields

Check for required frontmatter:
- [ ] `code:` present and valid (kebab-case, 2-20 chars)
- [ ] `name:` present
- [ ] `header:` present
- [ ] `subheader:` present
- [ ] `default_selected:` present (boolean)

### 3. Validate Custom Variables

For each custom variable:
- [ ] `prompt:` present
- [ ] `default:` present (or explicitly omitted)
- [ ] `result:` template valid
- [ ] Variable naming correct (kebab-case)

**For single-select:**
- [ ] `single-select:` array present
- [ ] All options have `value:` and `label:`

**For multi-select:**
- [ ] `multi-select:` array present
- [ ] All options have `value:` and `label:`

### 4. Validate Extension Module Code

**IF Extension:**
- [ ] `code:` matches base module code
- [ ] This is intentional (not an error)

### 5. Record Results

Append to `{validationReportOutput}`:

```markdown
## module.yaml Validation

**Status:** {PASS/FAIL/WARNINGS}

**Required Fields:** {status}
**Custom Variables:** {count} variables
**Issues Found:**
{list any issues}
```

### 6. Auto-Proceed

"**‚úì module.yaml check complete.**"

Proceeding to next validation...

Load `{nextStepFile}`

---

## Success Metrics

‚úÖ All module.yaml checks performed
‚úÖ Results recorded
‚úÖ Auto-proceeds to next validation

validationReportOutput: '{validation_report_output}'
targetPath: '{validation_target_path}'
---

# Step 4: Agent Specs Validation

## STEP GOAL:

Validate agent specifications and/or built agents, distinguishing between placeholder specs and fully implemented agents.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES:

### Universal Rules:
- üìñ CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- ‚úÖ Speak in `{communication_language}`

### Role Reinforcement:
- ‚úÖ You are the **Quality Assurance** ‚Äî dual-mode checking
- ‚úÖ Specs are expected, built agents are great
- ‚úÖ Track status of each agent

---

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Load Agent Files

Find all agent files in `{targetPath}/agents/`:
- `.spec.md` files (placeholder specs)
- `.agent.yaml` files (built agents)

### 2. Categorize Agents

For each agent found, determine status:

**Built Agents (.agent.yaml):**
- Full implementation with complete persona, menu YAML
- Can be validated in-depth via agent validation workflow

**Spec Agents (.spec.md):**
- High-level placeholder/blueprint
- Awaiting creation via agent-builder workflow

Track counts:
- Total agents: {count}
- Built agents: {count}
- Spec agents: {count}

### 3. Validate Spec Agents (.spec.md)

For each spec agent, check:

**Required Sections:**
- [ ] Agent metadata (id, name, title, icon, module)
- [ ] Role defined
- [ ] Identity or communication style
- [ ] Menu triggers documented
- [ ] hasSidecar decision documented

**Menu Triggers:**
- [ ] At least one trigger per agent
- [ ] Trigger ‚Üí workflow mapping clear
- [ ] No duplicate triggers (warn if found)

**hasSidecar Documentation:**
- [ ] Decision documented (true or false)
- [ ] Rationale if true (why memory needed)

**Placeholder Note:** These are specs awaiting agent-builder.

### 4. Validate Built Agents (.agent.yaml)

For each built agent, check:

**Frontmatter Completeness:**
- [ ] agent.metadata exists
- [ ] agent.persona exists
- [ ] agent.menu exists

**YAML Structure:**
- [ ] Valid YAML syntax
- [ ] Required fields present

**Status:** These are complete implementations and can be validated in detail via sub-process.

### 5. Record Results

Append to `{validationReportOutput}`:

```markdown
## Agent Specs Validation

**Status:** {PASS/FAIL/WARNINGS}

**Agent Summary:**
- Total Agents: {count}
- Built Agents: {count} {list}
- Spec Agents: {count} {list}

**Built Agents:**
{for each built agent}
- **{name}**: {status} - Ready for detailed validation via agent workflow

**Spec Agents:**
{for each spec agent}
- **{name}**: {status} - Placeholder awaiting agent-builder

**Issues Found:**
{list any issues}

**Recommendations:**
{if specs exist}
- Use `bmad:bmb:agents:agent-builder` to create {spec agent names}
- After building agents, re-run validation to verify compliance
{endif}
```

### 6. Note Sub-Process Opportunity

**IF built agents exist:**

"**The following built agents can be validated in detail:**"

{list built agents}

"**After this validation completes, I can spawn sub-processes to run the agent validation workflow on each built agent for deeper compliance checking.**"

### 7. Auto-Proceed

"**‚úì Agent specs check complete.**"

Proceeding to next validation...

Load `{nextStepFile}`

---

## Success Metrics

‚úÖ All agent files checked
‚úÖ Status tracked (spec vs built)
‚úÖ hasSidecar decisions validated
‚úÖ Recommendations for specs documented
‚úÖ Sub-process opportunity noted
